description: Lock Opera House Infrastructure to public opera venue systems at Teatro San Cassiano, not madrigal repertoire.

checks.reason: The first public opera house inherited public/commercial theater venue infrastructure, boxes, and civic construction oversight.
Public theater infrastructure is the venue lineage, not the opera-house technology itself.
San Cassiano opera staging used stage machinery and scene sets for spectacle.
Stage machinery enables production effects without being the whole venue system.
San Cassiano's public-opera model depended on paid audience entry.
Ticketing is the commercial public-access model, not a stage technology.
Madrigals and polyphony should not be restored unless the node is rescoped to musical genre lineage rather than opera-house infrastructure.
The node remains anchored to Teatro San Cassiano's 1637 public opera-house opening.
